name: Plain-Language Drill — Explaining Without Jargon
_note: Skill drill. Standard 4 metric keys re-pitched: medical_accuracy = correctness of the explanation, clinical_reasoning slot = clarity / jargon-free plain language (the drill's focus).

anchors.7-8: Occasional minor errors; meaning always clear

anchors.9-10: Every explanation is medically correct and complete enough
No unexplained jargon; uses everyday words and helpful analogies
anchors.7-8: Mostly plain; rare jargon, quickly clarified
anchors.5-6: Mixes in jargon that a patient might not follow
anchors.3-4: Relies on medical terms a patient would not understand
anchors.1-2: Explanation is as confusing as the original term

anchors.9-10: Exceptional empathy and professionalism; creates psychological safety
anchors.7-8: Good empathy; acknowledges emotion; polite and professional
anchors.5-6: Adequate but mechanical empathy; some emotional cues missed
anchors.3-4: Little empathic response; information delivery prioritized
anchors.1-2: No empathic response; dismissive or unprofessional

checklist.item: explained the term using everyday words (no new jargon)
used a comparison or analogy where helpful
kept the explanation short and focused
checked the patient's understanding (e.g. 'Does that make sense?')
used Teach-back method to verify patient understanding
